rules.description: fn+H/J/K/L → fn+Control+Arrows (TILING: macOS's native tiling shortcut is fn+control+arrow-keys; this lets you trigger it with vim-style hjkl instead — h=left, j=down, k=up, l=right)

manipulators.description: fn+H → fn+Control+Left Arrow (tile window LEFT half — native macOS tiling shortcut)
fn+J → fn+Control+Down Arrow (tile window BOTTOM half — native macOS tiling shortcut)
fn+K → fn+Control+Up Arrow (tile window TOP half — native macOS tiling shortcut)
fn+L → fn+Control+Right Arrow (tile window RIGHT half — native macOS tiling shortcut)

rules.description: fn+Control+H/J/K/L → fn+Control+Shift+Arrows (ARRANGING: macOS's native window-arranging shortcut is fn+control+shift+arrow-keys; this lets you trigger it with vim-style hjkl instead — h=left, j=down, k=up, l=right — one modifier lighter to type than the real shortcut)

manipulators.description: fn+Control+H → fn+Control+Shift+Left Arrow (arrange window LEFT — native macOS arranging shortcut)
fn+Control+J → fn+Control+Shift+Down Arrow (arrange window DOWN — native macOS arranging shortcut)
fn+Control+K → fn+Control+Shift+Up Arrow (arrange window UP — native macOS arranging shortcut)
fn+Control+L → fn+Control+Shift+Right Arrow (arrange window RIGHT — native macOS arranging shortcut)

rules.description: fn+Control+M → Command+M (MINIMIZE: triggers macOS's default 'Minimize' shortcut ⌘M — Minimize is left at its default binding in System Settings → Keyboard → Keyboard Shortcuts → App Shortcuts, this rule just lets fn+control+M trigger it too)

manipulators.description: fn+Control+M → Command+M (minimize the focused window — Apple's default Minimize shortcut)

rules.description: fn+M → Control+Option+Shift+M (MAXIMIZE: triggers the native macOS green-button 'Zoom' action — REQUIRES enabling 'Zoom' with shortcut ⌃⌥⇧M in System Settings → Keyboard → Keyboard Shortcuts → Window, this rule will not work without that rebind in place)

manipulators.description: fn+M → Control+Option+Shift+M (zoom/maximize the focused window — Apple's native Zoom shortcut, must be enabled at ⌃⌥⇧M in System Settings first)

rules.description: fn+C → fn+Control+C (CENTER: lets plain fn+C trigger your 'center window' shortcut without also holding Control)

manipulators.description: fn+C → fn+Control+C (center the focused window on screen)

rules.description: fn+F → Control+fn+F (FILL: lets plain fn+F trigger your 'fill screen' shortcut without also holding Control)

manipulators.description: fn+F → Control+fn+F (make the focused window fill the screen)

rules.description: fn+R → Control+fn+R (RESTORE: lets plain fn+R trigger your 'return window to previous size' shortcut without also holding Control)

manipulators.description: fn+R → Control+fn+R (restore the focused window to its previous size/position)
